questions.question: A cylindrical storage tank has a radius of 4 feet and a height of 10 feet. What is the volume of the tank in cubic feet? Use π ≈ 3.14.
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h. V = 3.14 × 4² × 10 = 3.14 × 16 × 10 = 502.4 cubic feet.

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 3 cm and a height of 8 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters? Use π ≈ 3.14.
A rectangular prism has dimensions 5 cm × 6 cm × 7 cm. A smaller rectangular prism with dimensions 2 cm × 3 cm × 4 cm is removed from it. What is the volume of the remaining solid?
questions.explanation: Volume of larger prism = 5 × 6 × 7 = 210 cm³. Volume of smaller prism = 2 × 3 × 4 = 24 cm³. Remaining = 210 − 24 = 186 cm³.

questions.question: Two spheres have radii of 2 inches and 4 inches. How many times greater is the volume of the larger sphere compared to the smaller sphere?
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere = (4/3)πr³. Since volume scales with the cube of the radius, the ratio is (4/4)³ = 2³ = 8.

questions.question: A triangular prism has a triangular base with a base of 6 meters and height of 4 meters. The prism extends 10 meters in length. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Area of triangular base = (1/2) × 6 × 4 = 12 m². Volume of prism = base area × length = 12 × 10 = 120 cubic meters.

questions.question: A cylinder has a volume of 360π cubic inches and a height of 10 inches. What is the radius of the cylinder?
questions.explanation: Volume of cylinder: πr²h = 360π. With h = 10: πr² × 10 = 360π. Therefore r² = 36, so r = 6 inches.
